title: Code of Conduct

title: Contributing to IncubationPeriodModels
text: can fix typos, spelling mistakes, grammatical errors documentation directly using GitHub web interface, long changes made source file. generally means ’ll need edit roxygen2 comments .R, .Rd file. can find .R file generates .Rd reading comment first line.

title: Contributing to IncubationPeriodModels
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed). See guide create great issue advice.

title: Contributing to IncubationPeriodModels
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("NyallJamieson/IncubationPeriodModels", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to IncubationPeriodModels
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use Air apply style, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Contributing to IncubationPeriodModels
text: Please note IncubationPeriodModels project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project agree abide terms.

what: Incubation periods in infectious disease epidemiology
title: Introduction to IncubationPeriodModels
text: incubation period time infection onset symptoms. Understanding delay important many epidemiological applications including outbreak investigation, transmission modelling, estimation key disease parameters. Incubation periods typically positive-valued right-skewed. Empirical estimates outbreak data often show substantial variability sometimes heavy tails. result, flexible parametric models often needed accurately represent distribution. Commonly used models include log-normal, gamma, Weibull distributions. However, models may always capture full range shapes observed incubation period data. Burr family distributions provides flexible alternative can represent wide range distributional shapes.

title: Introduction to IncubationPeriodModels
text: IncubationPeriodModels package implements several Burr-family distributions can used represent incubation period distributions. package currently provides implementations : Burr Type III Burr Type X Burr Type XII derived Burr-like distribution distributions allow flexible modelling right-skewed delay distributions can accommodate varying levels tail behaviour.

title: Introduction to IncubationPeriodModels
text: illustrate distributions can used, simulate incubation periods Burr Type XII distribution.

title: Introduction to IncubationPeriodModels
text: can visualise simulated data compare theoretical density.  example illustrates right-skewed distribution typically observed incubation periods.
code: hist(   incubation,   probability = TRUE,   breaks = 40,   main = "Simulated incubation periods",   xlab = "Time" )  curve(   dburr12(x, c = 2, k = 3, s = 1),   add = TRUE,   col = "red",   lwd = 2 )

title: Introduction to IncubationPeriodModels
text: One advantage Burr-family distributions allow wide range shapes depending parameter values.  Changing shape parameters modifies skewness tail behaviour distribution, allowing different incubation period profiles represented.
code: x <- seq(0, 10, length.out = 200)  plot(   x,   dburr12(x, c = 1.5, k = 2, s = 1),   type = "l",   lwd = 2,   ylab = "Density",   xlab = "Time",   main = "Effect of parameter changes" )  lines(x, dburr12(x, c = 2, k = 3, s = 1), col = 2, lwd = 2) lines(x, dburr12(x, c = 3, k = 4, s = 1), col = 3, lwd = 2)

title: Introduction to IncubationPeriodModels

title: Introduction to IncubationPeriodModels
text: Burr family distributions provides flexible parametric models right-skewed delay distributions incubation periods. IncubationPeriodModels package implements several Burr-family distributions R, allowing researchers easily simulate, visualise, analyse incubation period data using consistent interface.

code: @Manual{,   title = {IncubationPeriodModels: Burr Distribution Models for Incubation Period and Delay Analysis},   author = {Nyall Jamieson and Sam Abbott},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.1},   url = {https://github.com/NyallJamieson/IncubationPeriodModels}, }

title: IncubationPeriodModels
text: IncubationPeriodModels implements flexible Burr-family distributions modelling incubation periods epidemiological delay distributions. package provides density, distribution, quantile, random generation functions several Burr-family distributions derived Burr-like distribution. models offer flexible parametric representations positive-valued event times can accommodate wide range skewness, tail behaviour, hazard shapes. flexibility makes Burr distributions useful modelling incubation periods delay distributions encountered epidemiology heavy tails asymmetric shapes common.

title: IncubationPeriodModels
text: package currently provides implementations : Burr Type III Burr Type X Burr Type XII Derived Burr distribution distributions follow standard R distribution interface:

title: IncubationPeriodModels
text: Install development version GitHub: ```r # install.packages(“pak”) pak::pak(“NyallJamieson/IncubationPeriodModels”)

title: Burr type X distribution — burr10
text: Density, distribution function, quantile function random generation Burr type X distribution \(x>0\).

title: Burr type X distribution — burr10
code: dburr10(x, c, k, log = FALSE)  pburr10(q, c, k, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)  qburr10(p, c, k, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)  rburr10(n, c, k)

title: Burr type X distribution — burr10
text: x, q Numeric vector quantiles (>0). c Positive parameter. k Positive parameter. log Logical; TRUE return log-density. lower.tail Logical; TRUE (default) probabilities \(P[X \le x]\). log.p Logical; TRUE return log-probabilities. p Numeric vector probabilities 0 1. n Number observations.

title: Burr type XII distribution — burr12
text: Density, distribution function, quantile function random generation Burr type XII distribution \(x>0\).

title: Burr type XII distribution — burr12
code: dburr12(x, c, k, s, log = FALSE)  pburr12(q, c, k, s, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)  qburr12(p, c, k, s, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)  rburr12(n, c, k, s)

title: Burr type XII distribution — burr12
text: x, q Numeric vector quantiles (>0). c, k, s Positive parameters. log Logical; TRUE return log-density. lower.tail Logical; TRUE (default) probabilities \(P[X \le x]\). log.p Logical; TRUE return log-probabilities. p Numeric vector probabilities 0 1. n Number observations.

title: Burr type XII distribution — burr12
text: parameterization requires k > 1.

title: Burr type III distribution — burr3
text: Density, distribution function, quantile function random generation Burr type III distribution \(x>0\).

title: Burr type III distribution — burr3
code: dburr3(x, c, k, s, log = FALSE)  pburr3(q, c, k, s, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)  qburr3(p, c, k, s, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)  rburr3(n, c, k, s)

title: Burr type III distribution — burr3
text: x, q Numeric vector quantiles (>0). c, k, s Positive parameters. log Logical; TRUE return log-density. lower.tail Logical; TRUE (default) probabilities \(P[X \le x]\). log.p Logical; TRUE return log-probabilities. p Numeric vector probabilities 0 1. n Number observations.

title: Burr type III distribution — burr3
text: numeric vector.

title: Derived Burr-like distribution — burrNEW
text: Density, distribution function, quantile function random generation derived Burr-like distribution CDF $$F(x) = \left(1 + (T/x)^\exp((T-x)/b)\right)^{-1},\quad x>0.$$

title: Derived Burr-like distribution — burrNEW
code: dburrNEW(x, a, b, T, log = FALSE)  pburrNEW(q, a, b, T, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)  qburrNEW(   p,   a,   b,   T,   lower.tail = TRUE,   log.p = FALSE,   tol = 1e-10,   maxiter = 200 )  rburrNEW(n, a, b, T)

title: Derived Burr-like distribution — burrNEW
text: x, q Numeric vector quantiles (>0). , b, T Positive parameters. log Logical; TRUE return log-density. lower.tail Logical; TRUE (default) probabilities \(P[X \le x]\). log.p Logical; TRUE return log-probabilities. p Numeric vector probabilities 0 1. tol Numerical tolerance passed uniroot. maxiter Maximum iterations passed uniroot. n Number observations.

what: Burr distributions (Type III, X, XII) and a derived Burr-like distribution — burrdist
title: Burr distributions (Type III, X, XII) and a derived Burr-like distribution — burrdist
text: package implements density, distribution, quantile, random generation functions several Burr distributions derived Burr-like distribution.

title: Burr distributions (Type III, X, XII) and a derived Burr-like distribution — burrdist
text: Included families: Burr Type III: dburr3, pburr3, qburr3, rburr3 Burr Type X: dburr10, pburr10, qburr10, rburr10 Burr Type XII: dburr12, pburr12, qburr12, rburr12 Derived distribution: dburrNEW, pburrNEW, qburrNEW, rburrNEW distributions supported \(x > 0\).

what: Mean of the Burr type X distribution — meanburr10
title: Mean of the Burr type X distribution — meanburr10
text: Computes mean Burr type X distribution \(x>0\). mean computed numerically survival function.

title: Mean of the Burr type X distribution — meanburr10
Mean of the Burr type X distribution — meanburr10
text: c Positive parameter. k Positive parameter. rel.tol Relative tolerance passed integrate.

title: Mean of the Burr type X distribution — meanburr10
text: numeric value (may Inf NA finite / computable).

what: Mean of the Burr type XII distribution — meanburr12
title: Mean of the Burr type XII distribution — meanburr12
text: Mean Burr type XII distribution (parameterization requires k > 1). mean exists k > 1 + 1/c.

text: c, k, s Positive parameters.

what: Mean of the Burr type III distribution — meanburr3
title: Mean of the Burr type III distribution — meanburr3
text: Computes mean Burr type III distribution \(x>0\). mean computed numerically survival function: $$E[X] = \int_0^\infty (1 - F(x))\,dx.$$

Mean of the Burr type III distribution — meanburr3
text: c, k, s Positive parameters. rel.tol Relative tolerance passed integrate.

title: Mean of the Burr type III distribution — meanburr3
text: numeric value (may Inf NA finite / computable).
